2026-04-23T07:55:57.7805303Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T07:55:57.7805869Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T07:55:57.7806298Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T07:55:57.7806864Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T07:55:57.7807182Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T07:55:57.7807423Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T07:55:57.7807583Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-23T07:55:57.7807770Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T07:55:57.7807948Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-23T07:55:57.7808181Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-23T07:55:57.7808382Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T07:55:57.7808668Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-23T07:55:57.7808927Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). 2026-04-23T08:00:04.2855036Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T07:59:46.289644619Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/workloadssapvirtualinstance/Azure.ResourceManager.WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance/`\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #57924) by @ArcturusZhang\n\n## What Changed\n\nPR #57924 introduced the new `Azure.ResourceManager.WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance` package (version `1.0.0-beta.1`), which exposes four main resource types for managing SAP workloads on Azure: `SapVirtualInstance`, `SapApplicationServerInstance`, `SapCentralServerInstance`, and `SapDatabaseInstance`. The package includes auto-generated samples in `tests/Generated/Samples/` but has no service-specific documentation.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- **Key concepts section** is a single generic link — no description of the four core resource types (`SapVirtualInstance`, `SapApplicationServerInstance`, `SapCentralServerInstance`, `SapDatabaseInstance`) or what they represent\n- **Examples section** contains only a generic link to the Azure management samples catalog — no service-specific code snippets demonstrating CRUD operations on SAP Virtual Instances\n- **No `tests/Samples/` directory** — no snippet-backed sample test files exist; there are only auto-generated samples in `tests/Generated/Samples/` which cannot serve as README snippet sources\n- **README authentication section** is a redirect link only — no concrete `ArmClient` initialization example specific to this package\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n**Edit** `sdk/workloadssapvirtualinstance/Azure.ResourceManager.WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance/README.md`\n\nReplace the generic **Key concepts** section with:\n\n```markdown\n## Key concepts\n\n### SapVirtualInstance\n\n`SapVirtualInstanceResource` represents the top-level SAP system deployed on Azure. It encompasses the full SAP landscape — including the ABAP Central Services, application servers, and database — and is the entry point for managing the complete SAP workload.\n\n### SapApplicationServerInstance\n\n`SapApplicationServerInstanceResource` represents a single SAP Application Server (also called a dialog instance). An SAP Virtual Instance may contain one or more application server instances that handle end-user workloads.\n\n### SapCentralServerInstance\n\n`SapCentralServerInstanceResource` represents the ABAP Central Services instance (ASCS) within an SAP Virtual Instance. It hosts the message server and enqueue server, providing high-availability coordination.\n\n### SapDatabaseInstance\n\n`SapDatabaseInstanceResource` represents the database layer of an SAP Virtual Instance, such as an SAP HANA database instance.\n\n### Thread safety\n\nWe guarantee that all client instance methods are thread-safe and independent of each other ([guideline](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html#dotnet-service-methods-thread-safety)). This ensures that the recommendation of reusing client instances is always safe, even across threads.\n\n### Additional concepts\n\u003c!-- CLIENT COMMON BAR --\u003e\n[Client options](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#configuring-service-clients-using-clientoptions) |\n[Accessing the response](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#accessing-http-response-details-using-responset) |\n[Long-running operations](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#consuming-long-running-operations-using-operationt) |\n[Handling failures](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#reporting-errors-requestfailedexception) |\n[Diagnostics](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/samples/Diagnostics.md) |\n[Mocking](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/azure/sdk/unit-testing-mocking) |\n[Client lifetime](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/azure-sdk/lifetime-management-and-thread-safety-guarantees-of-azure-sdk-net-clients/)\n\u003c!-- CLIENT COMMON BAR --\u003e\n```\n\nReplace the generic **Examples** section with:\n\n```markdown\n## Examples\n\nThe following sections provide several code snippets covering some of the most common Azure Workloads SAP Virtual Instance tasks:\n\n- [Get a SAP Virtual Instance](#get-a-sap-virtual-instance)\n- [List all SAP Virtual Instances in a subscription](#list-all-sap-virtual-instances-in-a-subscription)\n- [List Application Server Instances](#list-application-server-instances)\n\n### Get a SAP Virtual Instance\n\n```C# Snippet:WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance_GetSapVirtualInstance\n```\n\n### List all SAP Virtual Instances in a subscription\n\n```C# Snippet:WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance_ListSapVirtualInstances\n```\n\n### List Application Server Instances\n\n```C# Snippet:WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance_ListApplicationServerInstances\n```\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\n**Create** `sdk/workloadssapvirtualinstance/Azure.ResourceManager.WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance/tests/Samples/Sample_SapVirtualInstance.cs` with the following content:\n\n```csharp\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\n\nusing System;\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\nusing Azure.Identity;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\nusing NUnit.Framework;\n\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance.Samples\n{\n    public class Sample_SapVirtualInstance\n    {\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only validating compilation of examples\")]\n        public async Task GetSapVirtualInstanceAsync()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance_GetSapVirtualInstance\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n\n            // Replace with your subscription ID, resource group name, and SAP Virtual Instance name\n            string subscriptionId = \"00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\";\n            string resourceGroupName = \"myResourceGroup\";\n            string sapVirtualInstanceName = \"mySapVirtualInstance\";\n\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client\n                .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($\"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\"))\n                .GetResourceGroupAsync(resourceGroupName);\n\n            SapVirtualInstanceResource sapVirtualInstance = await resourceGroup\n                .GetSapVirtualInstances()\n                .GetAsync(sapVirtualInstanceName);\n\n            Console.WriteLine($\"SAP Virtual Instance: {sapVirtualInstance.Data.Name}\");\n            Console.WriteLine($\"Environment: {sapVirtualInstance.Data.Environment}\");\n            Console.WriteLine($\"SAP Product: {sapVirtualInstance.Data.SapProduct}\");\n            #endregion\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only validating compilation of examples\")]\n        public async Task ListSapVirtualInstancesAsync()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance_ListSapVirtualInstances\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n\n            string subscriptionId = \"00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\";\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client\n                .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($\"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\"))\n                .GetAsync();\n\n            await foreach (SapVirtualInstanceResource svi in subscription.GetSapVirtualInstancesAsync())\n            {\n                Console.WriteLine($\"Name: {svi.Data.Name}, Location: {svi.Data.Location}\");\n            }\n            #endregion\n        }\n\n        [Test]\n        [Ignore(\"Only validating compilation of examples\")]\n        public async Task ListApplicationServerInstancesAsync()\n        {\n            #region Snippet:WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance_ListApplicationServerInstances\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\n\n            string subscriptionId = \"00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000\";\n            string resourceGroupName = \"myResourceGroup\";\n            string sapVirtualInstanceName = \"mySapVirtualInstance\";\n\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await client\n                .GetSubscriptionResource(new ResourceIdentifier($\"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}\"))\n                .GetResourceGroupAsync(resourceGroupName);\n\n            SapVirtualInstanceResource sapVirtualInstance = await resourceGroup\n                .GetSapVirtualInstances()\n                .GetAsync(sapVirtualInstanceName);\n\n            await foreach (SapApplicationServerInstanceResource appServer in sapVirtualInstance.GetSapApplicationServerInstancesAsync())\n            {\n                Console.WriteLine($\"App Server: {appServer.Data.Name}, Status: {appServer.Data.Status}\");\n            }\n            #endregion\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\nFor each snippet:\n| Snippet name | Test file | README location |\n|---|---|---|\n| `WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance_GetSapVirtualInstance` | `tests/Samples/Sample_SapVirtualInstance.cs` | Under `### Get a SAP Virtual Instance` |\n| `WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance_ListSapVirtualInstances` | `tests/Samples/Sample_SapVirtualInstance.cs` | Under `### List all SAP Virtual Instances in a subscription` |\n| `WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance_ListApplicationServerInstances` | `tests/Samples/Sample_SapVirtualInstance.cs` | Under `### List Application Server Instances` |\n\n### Step 3: Verify README 2026-04-23T08:00:04.3224671Z server:unified callBackendTool: serverID=safeoutputs, toolName=create_issue, args=map[body:## Documentation Gap
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3224681Z 
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3224874Z **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance`
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3225223Z **Service directory:** `sdk/workloadssapvirtualinstance/Azure.ResourceManager.WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance/`
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3225484Z **Triggered by:** commit 1b854213504f49440f2dede6631cc3a35fe71571 (PR #57924) by @ArcturusZhang
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3225488Z 
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3225567Z ## What Changed
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3225576Z 
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3227000Z PR #57924 introduced the new `Azure.ResourceManager.WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance` package (version `1.0.0-beta.1`), which exposes four main resource types for managing SAP workloads on Azure: `SapVirtualInstance`, `SapApplicationServerInstance`, `SapCentralServerInstance`, and `SapDatabaseInstance`. The package includes auto-generated samples in `tests/Generated/Samples/` but has no service-specific documentation.
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3227011Z 
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3227093Z ## Gaps Found
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3227097Z 
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3227977Z - **Key concepts section** is a single generic link — no description of the four core resource types (`SapVirtualInstance`, `SapApplicationServerInstance`, `SapCentralServerInstance`, `SapDatabaseInstance`) or what they represent
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3228650Z - **Examples section** contains only a generic link to the Azure management samples catalog — no service-specific code snippets demonstrating CRUD operations on SAP Virtual Instances
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3229479Z - **No `tests/Samples/` directory** — no snippet-backed sample test files exist; there are only auto-generated samples in `tests/Generated/Samples/` which cannot serve as README snippet sources
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3229959Z - **README authentication section** is a redirect link only — no concrete `ArmClient` initialization example specific to this package
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3229972Z 
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3230163Z <details>
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3230386Z <summary><strong>📐 Implementation Guide</strong></summary>
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3230391Z 
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3230709Z This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3230714Z 
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3230802Z ### Step 1: Modify files
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3230806Z 
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3231134Z **Edit** `sdk/workloadssapvirtualinstance/Azure.ResourceManager.WorkloadsSapVirtualInstance/README.md`
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3231138Z 
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3231266Z Replace the generic **Key concepts** section with:
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3231270Z 
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3231342Z ```markdown
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3231423Z ## Key concepts
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3231427Z 
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3231511Z ### SapVirtualInstance
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3231515Z 
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3240891Z `SapVirtualInstanceResource` represents the top-level SAP system deployed on Azure. It encompasses the full SAP landscape — including the ABAP Central Services, application servers, and database — and is the entry point for managing the complete SAP workload.
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3240912Z 
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3241047Z ### SapApplicationServerInstance
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3241052Z 
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3241814Z `SapApplicationServerInstanceResource` represents a single SAP Application Server (also called a dialog instance). An SAP Virtual Instance may contain one or more application server instances that handle end-user workloads.
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3241819Z 
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3241916Z ### SapCentralServerInstance
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3241921Z 
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3242619Z `SapCentralServerInstanceResource` represents the ABAP Central Services instance (ASCS) within an SAP Virtual Instance. It hosts the message server and enqueue server, providing high-availability coordination.
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3242624Z 
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3242714Z ### SapDatabaseInstance
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3242904Z 
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3243310Z `SapDatabaseInstanceResource` represents the database layer of an SAP Virtual Instance, such as an SAP HANA database instance.
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3243315Z 
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3243394Z ### Thread safety
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3243398Z 
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3244433Z We guarantee that all client instance methods are thread-safe and independent of each other ([guideline](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html#dotnet-service-methods-thread-safety)). This ensures that the recommendation of reusing client instances is always safe, even across threads.
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3244437Z 
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3244524Z ### Additional concepts
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3244610Z <!-- CLIENT COMMON BAR -->
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3245106Z [Client options](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#configuring-service-clients-using-clientoptions) |
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3245646Z [Accessing the response](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#accessing-http-response-details-using-responset) |
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3246193Z [Long-running operations](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#consuming-long-running-operations-using-operationt) |
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3246661Z [Handling failures](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#reporting-errors-requestfailedexception) |
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3246998Z [Diagnostics](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/samples/Diagnostics.md) |
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3247220Z [Mocking](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/azure/sdk/unit-testing-mocking) |
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3247698Z [Client lifetime](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/azure-sdk/lifetime-management-and-thread-safety-guarantees-of-azure-sdk-net-clients/)
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3247789Z <!-- CLIENT COMMON BAR -->
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3247857Z ```
2026-04-23T08:00:04.3247862Z
